raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The delegation asks to make the draft stronger, which centrally includes improving the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement.","R1.2":"Strengthening the artifact requires checking whether the argument from problem motivation to method to claimed contribution holds together.","R1.3":"Because the passage relies on benchmark results to justify the method, claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.","R1.4":"The draft makes a first-algorithm claim and positions itself against prior work, so novelty assessment is central.","R1.5":"Improving strength may require section- or paragraph-level reordering of the narrative arc in the presented draft.","R1.6":"A request to make prose stronger centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish, emphasis, and concision.","RX.1":"Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of strengthening is intended by the broad delegation.","RX.2":"The projection must account for avoiding inflated novelty, performance, or practical-impact claims.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within the requested strengthening task and not expand into executing unrelated review or research work.","RX.4":"The projection must account for avoiding changes that could mislead readers about scientific validity or applicability.","RX.5":"The projection must preserve traceability to the artifact's stated claims, citations, and reported results."}}

rationale.R1.1: The delegation asks to make the draft stronger, which centrally includes improving the thesis, contribution framing, and gap statement.
rationale.R1.2: Strengthening the artifact requires checking whether the argument from problem motivation to method to claimed contribution holds together.
rationale.R1.3: Because the passage relies on benchmark results to justify the method, claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: The draft makes a first-algorithm claim and positions itself against prior work, so novelty assessment is central.
rationale.R1.5: Improving strength may require section- or paragraph-level reordering of the narrative arc in the presented draft.
rationale.R1.6: A request to make prose stronger centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level polish, emphasis, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of strengthening is intended by the broad delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must account for avoiding inflated novelty, performance, or practical-impact claims.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within the requested strengthening task and not expand into executing unrelated review or research work.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for avoiding changes that could mislead readers about scientific validity or applicability.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must preserve traceability to the artifact's stated claims, citations, and reported results.
